description_long: This is a classic cylindrical fire hydrant with a domed top and a flanged base, finished in a weathered, dark red paint with visible signs of rust and wear. The hydrant features two side hose connection outlets, each capped and secured with metal chains, and a central front outlet with a similar cap and chain. The top of the hydrant has a prominent operating nut or cap, used for opening the water valve. The body is constructed from heavy cast metal, likely iron or steel, and the surface shows a rough, matte texture with patches of corrosion. The chains and caps are metallic, adding functional detail and authenticity. No manufacturer or model markings are visible on the exterior.
description: A weathered, red cylindrical fire hydrant with three capped outlets and metal chains, showing rust and a rough, matte finish.
description_view_0: The hydrant is seen from an angle, highlighting the front outlet with its cap and chain, as well as one side outlet and the domed top.
description_view_1: A direct front view showing all three capped outlets, with chains attached to each cap and the operating nut visible on top.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0 but from the opposite angle, showing the other side outlet and the front outlet with their respective chains.
description_view_3: A rear view displaying the back of the hydrant, both side outlets, and the domed top, with chains visible but the front outlet obscured.
